name: Interviewer entered the wrong candidate name
description: The recruiter typed 'Amit Shah' but the real candidate is 'Amit Sharma'. The email local-part and behavior must override the incorrect name metadata.

metadata.calendarInviteText: Interview for Amit Shah (amit.sharma@example.com) with Priya Mehta and Rohan Gupta.

payload.text: Thanks for joining. Let's start — can you introduce yourself?
Hi, my name is Amit Sharma. I am currently a backend developer and I worked on payment gateway integrations.
Tell me about the toughest integration you handled.
I solved a double-charge bug in a UPI flow — my project processed refunds idempotently after that.
Could you walk me through how you would design webhook retries?
I would approach it with exponential backoff and a dead-letter queue. During my internship I implemented exactly this.

notes: 'Amit Sharma' vs metadata name 'Amit Shah' only reaches the shared-token tier — but the exact email match and candidate-style answers resolve the conflict.
Expected outcome: p2 selected; evidence surfaces the name/email conflict instead of hiding it.
